message:
perf(mvcc): skip waiter-shard mutex on post-park wake fast path

In InProcessPageLockTable::wait_for_holder_change, when the holder has
changed after park_timeout the release side's notify_waiters_for_page
already drained our entry from the waiter queue via map.remove(&page).
The existing code called unregister_waiter unconditionally, taking the
waiter-shard mutex for no work. Skip that acquire when holder !=
observed_holder.

The spurious-wake / timeout branch still calls unregister_waiter so the
next iteration's register_waiter does not duplicate the thread in the
queue.

Before: 78,458 wakes/sec (N=5 trials, median; ±0.6% variance)
After:  78,362 wakes/sec (N=5 trials, median; ±0.8% variance)
Bench:  test_in_process_lock_table_wait_for_holder_change_microbench
        (added here; 6-consumer producer/consumer with 30µs producer
        hold to force the park path)
Cmd:    rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc4_mvcc \
        cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --release \
        test_in_process_lock_table_wait_for_holder_change_microbench \
        -- --ignored --nocapture

The 6-consumer microbench does not reproduce the MT-8t waiter-shard
contention that showed unregister_waiter at 3.00% self-time in
memory/session_2026_04_23_profiling_campaign.md; delta is within
variance on this uncontended-mutex bench. The change is a pure
subtraction of work (one saved mutex acquire per successful wake) and
keeps all four existing wait-path correctness tests passing:
  - test_in_process_lock_table_wait_for_holder_change_wakes_on_release
  - test_in_process_lock_table_wait_for_holder_change_times_out
  - test_targeted_wake_correct_thread
  - test_no_spurious_wakes

Follow-up: validate under fsqlite-e2e mt_mvcc_bench MT 8t writes where
the profile signal was captured.

Bead: bd-xeaze

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
